title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in responsive desktop layout and resize policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.
Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.
Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Layout Manager Scope
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug puzzle team handles case 1002 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Small Window Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-small-window-recovery-6bdrq3" that uses case 2443 to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Small Window Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Small Window Recovery into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Small Window Recovery, keep Small Window Recovery connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-small-window-recovery-6bdrq3
# Competency: maze-small-window-recovery.
# Case 2443: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_small_window_recovery_6bdrq3(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_6bdrq3 = "6bdrq3-3qrdb6-2443"
    assert evidence_variant_6bdrq3
    evidence_axes_6bdrq3 = (
        "grid6bdrq3",
        "topology2443",
        "search3qrdb6",
        "event6bdrq32443",
        "access24436bdrq3",
        "recovery3qrdb62443",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_6bdrq3)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"
